subject: Chemistry
solution:
Dehydration of alcohols involves the formation of carbocation intermediate and this carbocation rearrange to form more stable carbocation which looses proton to form a more stable alkene.

In the following dehydration reaction also initially a secondary carbocation is formed which undergoes methyl shift to form a more stable tertiary carbocation which looses proton to form a stable alkene.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
solution:
Non-metal oxides are generally acidic in nature.

Cl2O7- Acidic

Metal oxides are generally basic in nature

Na2O- Basic

Amphoteric oxides reacts with both acids and bases. 

Al2O3- Amphoteric.

Some non-metal oxides don't react with both acids and bases they are neutral in nature.

N2O- Netural

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Complexes in which central atom has unpaired electrons is said to be paramagnetic in nature and where unpaired electrons are not present are diamagnetic in nature.

FeCN63- ,   Fe+3  3d5 → t2g2,2  eg1, paramagnetic (one unpaired electron)

FeCN64-,  Fe+2 3d6→t2g6eg0 ,diamagnetic 

TiCN63-,  Ti+3(3d1)→t2g1 , Paramagnetic (1 unpaired electron)

NiCN42-, Ni+2(3d8)→ Diamagnetic

CoCN63-,   Co+3(3d6)→t2g6, diamagnetic

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Cis-trans isomerism is a type of geometrical isomerism.This type of isomerism occurs when there is a change in spatial arrangements of ligands. Cis and trans are the two terms used for representing the position of identical ligands when they are placed adjacent and opposite

(i) CoCl3.4NH3⇒CoNH34Cl2Cl, can exhibit cis-trans isomerism.

(ii) CoCl3.5NH3⇒CoNH35ClCl2,can't exhibit cis-trans  isomerism.

(iii) CoCl3.6NH3⇒CoNH36Cl3,can't exhibit cis-trans  isomerism.

(IV)

 CoClNO32.5NH3⇒CoNH35ClNO32 or CoNH35NO3Cl NO3

Both can't exhibit cis-trans  isomerism.

Hence. only one complex exhibits cis-trans isomerism

solution:
Photochemical smog occurs in warm, dry and sunny climate.

Photochemical smog results from the action of sunlight on unsaturated hydro carbons and nitrogen oxide.

The common components of photochemical smog are ozone, nitric oxide, acrolein, formaldehyde and peroxyacetyl nitrate (PAN).

It is oxidising smog.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Aldehydic (-CHO) group has greater priority than keto group and nitro groups is always a prefix.

Root word of the given compound is hept, double bond is the primary suffix and aldehydic group becomes secondary suffix and keto and nitro groups are prefixes.

Hence, the correct IUPAC name of the given compound is

4-Methyl-6-nitro-3-oxohept-4-enal

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
During extraction of copper, iron oxide present as impurity is removed by adding SiO2 and converting it into FeSiO3

FeO+SiO2→FeSiO3          Flux          Slag

Hence,FeO is gangue and FeSiO3 is slag.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Isotopes are the atoms of the same element with different mass number. They have the same atomic number. So, number of protons and electrons are same, Hence their electronic configuration are same, but number of neutrons are different.

Hydrogen has three isotopes: protium, H11, deuterium, H12 or D and tritium, H13 or T.  These isotopes differ from one another in respect of the presence of neutrons. Ordinary hydrogen, protium, has no neutrons, deuterium (also known as heavy hydrogen) has one and tritium has two neutrons in the nucleus.

solution:
Natural rubber

Natural rubber is a polymer of isoprene, and obtained from natural source-latex tree. In natural rubber, isoprene units are joined together in head-to-tail fashion and all double bonds in the polymer chain have cis configurations as shown in the given figure.

The polymer contains cis repeating units and has a molecular weight ranging from 100,000 upto 1,000,000

The cis arrangement of the double bonds in natural rubber prevents the rubber molecules from fitting into an ordered structure. Thus, rubber is an amorphous polymer. Because of the random coiling of its polymer chains, rubber stretches easily. When stretched, the rubber molecules are forced into a higher energy state. when the tension is released, rubber snaps back to its original random coiled state but it is nonpolar therefore statement-II is incorrect.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Oxides of metals are generally basic in nature but some are amphoteric in nature.

Oxides of non-metals are acidic in nature but some of them are neutral.

SO3 is acidic in nature it reacts with bases.

Al2O3 is amphoteric in nature as it reacts with both acids and bases.

SiO2 is acidic in nature.

CaO is a basic oxide and it reacts with acids readily.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Colloidal particles always carry an electric charge. The nature of this charge is the same on all the particles in a given colloidal solution and may be either positive or negative. 

Negatively charged sol ⇒CdS

Positively charged sol ⇒Fe2O3.xH2O

Macromolecular colloids are individual particles that are large enough to be considered colloids (diameter around 100 nm). These particles are made up of chemicals with a high molecular weight.

Macromolecular colloid ⇒Starch

Gel ⇒ Cheese

solution:
Finding the cases for fa,b=fb,a≥a we have,

1,1,1,4,4,1,2,4,4,2,3,4,4,3,4,4- all have one choice for image.

2,1,1,2,2,2- all have three choices for image.

3,2,2,3,3,1,1,3,3,3-all have two choices for image.

So the total functions =3×3×2×2×2=72

Case 1: None of the pre-images have 3 as image. 

Total functions =2×2×1×1×1=4

Case 2: None of the pre-images have 2 as image

Total functions =2×2×2×2×2=32

Case 3: None of the pre-images have either 3 or 2 as image

Total functions =1×1×1×1×1=1

∴ Total onto functions =72-4-32+1=37

Now, ∑n=150Sn+2n+1-n-1

From equation i, 

=∑n=150n2+1-2n+2+2n+1-n-1

=∑n=150n2-n+2∑n=1501n+1-1n+2

=∑n=150n2-∑n=150n+212-13+13-14+⋯+151-152

=50511016-50512+212-152

=2517101-2551+5052

=251717-51+5052  =251666+2526

=41650+2526

∴126+∑n=150Sn+2n+1-n-1=41650+1=41651

subject: Mathematics
solution:
To check if R1 is transitive -

Let a=16, b=4, c=1

Here a-b=16-4≤13 holds true

now b-c=4−1≤13 ⇒3⩽13   also holds true

but a-c=16−1≰13

Hence R1 is not transitive

Similarly, to check transitivity of R2

Let a=16, b=4, c=3

Here a-b=16-4≠13 and b-c=4-3≠13

but a-c=16-3=13

Hence, R2 is also not transitive.

Hence neither R1 nor R2 is an equivalence relation

solution:

i=VR=2205=44 A.

The voltage across the inductor is double that across the resistor. Therefore, VL=2VR=440 V.

We know that VL=iXL=i×2πfL⇒L=44044×100π=110π H=100πmH

Therefore, K=1100=0.01≈0.

subject: Physics
solution:
Each pair on the right side of the circuit is in parallel connection, hence it can be replaced by a resistance of r2 as shown.

Now, r & r2 in each branch on the right side is in series, hence combined resistance value will be, 3r2. Upper branch having resistance 3r2 is in parallel with another 3r2, hence it can be written as 3r4.

Further, simplified circuit will have net resistance of 7r8.

Therefore, Req=7r8+r=78+1=158 Ω

Now, current I=3158=85 A

Hence, value of a=8.

subject: Physics
solution:
\(\mathrm{P}=\frac{\varepsilon^2}{R}\)
where, \(\varepsilon=\) inducedemf \(=-\frac{d \phi}{d t}\)
\(\phi=\mathrm{NBA}\)
\(\varepsilon=\mathrm{NA} \frac{\mathrm{dB}}{\mathrm{dt}}\)
also, \(R \propto \frac{1}{\mathrm{r}^2}\)
where, \(\mathrm{R}=\) resistance and \(\mathrm{r}=\) radius of wire
\(\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{P} \propto \frac{\mathrm{N}^2 \mathrm{r}^2}{\mathrm{H}^2} \\ & \frac{\mathrm{P}_1}{\mathrm{P}_2}=1 \\ & \mathrm{P}_1=\mathrm{P}_2 \end{aligned}\)
This means if the number of turns were quadrupled and the wire radius halved, the electrical power dissipated would be remains same.

When O2 dissociates into O its molecular mass becomes M2. Therefore,

Ratio of RMS speed, v1v2=T1T2×M2M1⇒v1v2=T2T×M/2M⇒v2v1=2
